description: Test corpus for the secure-code-review skill. Each fixture is a classic injectable code pattern this review checklist would flag; schema-driven means the runner smoke-validates shape while detect marks code that violates a NEVER (eval of user input, command injection, SSRF, SQL string-building) and ignore marks the safe form (allowlists, parameterized queries, no shell, validated outbound URLs).

fixtures.reason: User-controlled input is passed straight to eval(), allowing arbitrary code execution. Violates the NEVER 'Pass user input directly to eval'. CWE-95 / CWE-94 Code Injection.

User input is concatenated into a shell command run by child_process.exec; 'host=8.8.8.8; rm -rf /' executes attacker commands. Violates the NEVER 'Pass user input directly to child_process'. CWE-78 OS Command Injection.

An attacker-supplied URL is fetched server-side with no scheme/host allowlist, letting them reach internal services and the cloud metadata endpoint. Fails the ALWAYS 'Validate input at the trust boundary'. CWE-918 SSRF.

Input is validated to be a literal IP, and execFile passes args as a vector with no shell, so no metacharacters are interpreted. Follows the ALWAYS input-validation rule; no command injection. CWE-78 mitigated.
Uses a parameterized query (bound placeholder), so user input cannot alter SQL structure. Follows the ALWAYS 'Use parameterized queries' rule; no SQL injection. CWE-89 mitigated.
Outbound URL is constrained to https and a host allowlist before the request, blocking access to internal/metadata addresses. Follows the ALWAYS trust-boundary validation rule; SSRF mitigated.
